jokes.text: Don't you mean "Brotherly Love"? / Take my word on this one, Carlton.
jokes.setup: Carlton corrects Will's malapropism about Philadelphia's nickname
jokes.punchline: Will's confident dismissal implies he knows something Carlton doesn't about the city
jokes.explanation: Classic Will/Carlton dynamic with Will's street knowledge vs Carlton's book learning. Economical setup/punchline.

jokes.text: No country club reciprocity.
jokes.setup: Will describes the fun activities in Philadelphia
jokes.punchline: Carlton's only concern is the lack of country club privileges
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific humor. Four words perfectly encapsulate Carlton's privileged worldview in contrast to Will's street activities.

jokes.text: God forbid she should send a picture.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil mentions Aunt Vy wants to show off her redecorating
jokes.punchline: Carlton's sarcastic suggestion that a photo would suffice
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic complaint fitting Carlton's character but not particularly inventive.

jokes.text: It is a damn shame when people are spoiled by money.
jokes.setup: Carlton complaining about not going to a fancy resort
jokes.punchline: Will's ironic observation given that he's also now living in luxury
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment - Will calls out spoiled behavior while being somewhat spoiled himself.

jokes.text: G, put some butter on that, bring that out to me in the car.
jokes.setup: Will just criticized people being spoiled by money
jokes.punchline: Immediately acts spoiled by ordering Geoffrey around casually
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback showing Will's hypocrisy - criticizes spoiled behavior then immediately acts spoiled.

jokes.text: I spent 10 years customizing this thing to my boogie.
jokes.setup: Aunt Vy shows off her TV chair
jokes.punchline: The phrase 'customizing to my boogie' - personalizing furniture to body shape
jokes.explanation: 'Customizing to my boogie' is a colorful, memorable phrase that captures working-class furniture attachment.

jokes.text: Now, I don't want none of y'all sitting in it and stretching out my sweet spot.
jokes.setup: Continuation of the chair customization joke
jokes.punchline: 'Sweet spot' - treating a chair like a baseball bat or golf club
jokes.explanation: Extension of the chair joke with sports terminology applied to furniture comfort.

jokes.text: Don't make me kill you in your mother's home.
jokes.setup: Warning about the chair
jokes.punchline: Threatening murder but being polite about the location
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with polite consideration - threatens death but acknowledges the impropriety of the location.

jokes.text: Yeah. I mean it's so... poor.
jokes.setup: Asked to compliment Aunt Vy's redecorating
jokes.punchline: Hilary's oblivious classist observation delivered as a compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - her oblivious elitism delivered with complete sincerity creates cringe comedy.

jokes.text: What's Happening! Or What's Happening Now? / What's Happening! / Neato! I get to be Rerun.
jokes.setup: Will finds his old board game
jokes.punchline: Carlton's enthusiastic knowledge of the show and desire to play the fat character
jokes.explanation: Carlton's unexpected enthusiasm for playing Rerun shows his quirky side and creates mild irony.

jokes.text: Hey, Mom, listen, you should probably make sure that Uncle Phil sleeps on the bottom bunk. Because if he get up on the top bunk... Well, you know gravity ain't no joke. You know what I'm saying? He could end up in China.
jokes.setup: Sleeping arrangements being discussed
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil is so heavy he'd crash through to China
jokes.explanation: Standard fat joke with creative 'dig to China' escalation, but somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: How about if just I left?
jokes.setup: Family discussing hotel vs staying with Aunt Vy
jokes.punchline: Carlton's hopeful attempt to escape alone
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character moment - Carlton's desperation delivered matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: That's what I get for being considerate.
jokes.setup: Carlton's selfish request to leave alone
jokes.punchline: He frames his selfishness as 'consideration'
jokes.explanation: Good ironic self-awareness - Carlton reframes his selfish desire as virtue.

jokes.text: You're cheating. You're cheating. My mama cheating.
jokes.setup: Will challenges Aunt Vy to race but she gets head start while he ties shoes
jokes.punchline: Will's playful accusation of cheating
jokes.explanation: Playful family dynamic showing Will's comfort with Aunt Vy, but fairly standard humor.

jokes.text: How come we never had that much fun? / I don't know. / What's on TV?
jokes.setup: Carlton observes Will and Aunt Vy's playful relationship
jokes.punchline: Hilary's immediate dismissal and pivot to TV
jokes.explanation: Shows the contrast between Will's natural family connection and the Banks family's more distant relationships.

jokes.text: Right about now, I bet Geoffrey is turning back my covers and putting a little mint on my pillow.
jokes.setup: Hilary missing home comforts
jokes.punchline: Her specific expectation of hotel-level service at home
jokes.explanation: Standard Hilary entitlement joke - expecting luxury service even from household staff.

jokes.text: And your feet.
jokes.setup: Will says the food 'sticks to your ribs'
jokes.punchline: Carlton's observation that it also sticks to your feet (implying greasy floor)
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about greasy spoon restaurants. Economical and well-timed.

jokes.text: What do you have to tip the maitre d' to get a good stool?
jokes.setup: Carlton at a working-class sandwich shop
jokes.punchline: Applies fine dining etiquette to a simple lunch counter
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton fish-out-of-water humor - treating a deli like a fancy restaurant.

jokes.text: Why don't you take your Bing Crosby butt over to the golden arches and lie down in front of a drive-through window.
jokes.setup: Carlton complains about customer service
jokes.punchline: Duke's elaborate insult combining old-school reference with McDonald's
jokes.explanation: Creative insult with unexpected Bing Crosby reference plus vivid McDonald's imagery. Memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: Four Joe Frazier's in cheese trunks.
jokes.setup: Will orders cheese steaks
jokes.punchline: Duke's boxing-themed menu terminology
jokes.explanation: Clever Philadelphia-specific wordplay connecting local boxing legend to sandwich orders.

jokes.text: Wrap one in fungus.
jokes.setup: Request for mushrooms on sandwich
jokes.punchline: Duke's unappetizing but technically accurate description
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to establish Duke's colorful ordering system. 'Fungus' is both accurate and unappetizing.

jokes.text: You call that pathetic strip of graffiti-littered concrete legendary?
jokes.setup: Will points out the legendary basketball court
jokes.punchline: Carlton's harsh but accurate description of urban decay
jokes.explanation: Carlton's privileged perspective clashing with Will's nostalgic view creates character-based humor.

jokes.text: How come I got older, but you didn't?
jokes.setup: Will recognizes Dick Clark
jokes.punchline: Jokes about Dick Clark's famous agelessness
jokes.explanation: Standard reference to Dick Clark's youthful appearance - well-known celebrity joke but well-delivered.

jokes.text: Duke's sandwiches, a fountain of youth.
jokes.setup: Question about Dick Clark's agelessness
jokes.punchline: Dick Clark plays along by crediting the sandwich shop
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity cameo humor - Dick Clark playing along with the joke about his appearance.

jokes.text: One Will Smith.
jokes.setup: Dick Clark orders a chicken sandwich
jokes.punchline: Duke reveals a sandwich named after someone who ran away
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup for the episode's central conflict. Meta-humor about the character's past that drives the plot.

jokes.text: Only instead of fighting, he ran clear across the country to California. He was a chicken to go.
jokes.setup: Explaining the Will Smith sandwich name
jokes.punchline: 'Chicken to go' - both literally chicken sandwich and coward who left
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double meaning pun that perfectly captures the episode's central conflict. 'Chicken to go' works on multiple levels.

jokes.text: The ears were a little bigger, but it is you!
jokes.setup: Duke recognizing Will Smith
jokes.punchline: Backhanded recognition focusing on Will's prominent ears
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy about Will Smith's ears, somewhat cheap but character-appropriate for Duke.

jokes.text: Hey, it's got a good beat, and you can run to it.
jokes.setup: Duke making Will 'strut' like a chicken
jokes.punchline: Dick Clark's reference to American Bandstand while mocking Will's running away
jokes.explanation: Clever reference to Dick Clark's famous American Bandstand phrase twisted to mock Will's situation.

jokes.text: California's the other way!
jokes.setup: Will runs away from embarrassment
jokes.punchline: Duke's mocking direction about which way California is
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the running away theme, though somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: That's funny? / I ain't laughing.
jokes.setup: Will's friends presumably laughing at his reputation
jokes.punchline: Will's serious response contrasted with friend's deadpan denial
jokes.explanation: Standard comedic tension between Will's sensitivity and his friends' casual attitude.

jokes.text: We can't. All our relatives live here.
jokes.setup: Friends claim they don't run from fights
jokes.punchline: Practical reason they can't run - family ties
jokes.explanation: Smart observational humor about urban community ties - they can't run because everyone knows where they live.

jokes.text: I just went to visit y'all for four years.
jokes.setup: Will defending against accusations of running away
jokes.punchline: Reframing his move as an extended visit
jokes.explanation: Good character defense mechanism - Will's attempt to reframe his situation with obvious absurdity.

jokes.text: Hey, mister, there's nothing worse than dirty Dockers.
jokes.setup: Carlton apparently needs the bathroom
jokes.punchline: His concern about soiling his preppy pants brand
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - even in crisis, focused on brand-name clothing.

jokes.text: The dude that be spinning me over his head in the opening credits.
jokes.setup: Explaining who Omar is
jokes.punchline: Reference to the show's opening sequence
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor breaking the fourth wall by referencing the show's iconic opening sequence.

jokes.text: Yo, remember in junior high when he stole the principal's car just to run him over?
jokes.setup: Establishing how tough Omar is
jokes.punchline: Absurdly violent backstory escalating the threat
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalating Omar's reputation to absurd levels. Good escalation structure.

jokes.text: If I win, I need a witness. If I lose, you're my blood type.
jokes.setup: Will explaining why he needs Carlton's help
jokes.punchline: Carlton as witness vs. blood donor depending on outcome
jokes.explanation: Excellent parallel structure joke with dark humor. Perfectly economical setup/punchline construction.

jokes.text: Gee, this playground makes the other one look like Club Med.
jokes.setup: Carlton seeing an even worse playground
jokes.punchline: Comparison to luxury resort highlights urban decay
jokes.explanation: Carlton's privileged perspective creating humor through inappropriate luxury comparisons.

jokes.text: A bad smell isn't worth a mouthful of bloody Chicklets.
jokes.setup: Will talks about reputation following you like a bad smell
jokes.punchline: Carlton's graphic description using candy brand for teeth
jokes.explanation: Creative use of brand name to describe dental damage. Vivid and memorable imagery.

jokes.text: It's like his brain is so tiny, if he shake his big head like this, it'd sound like a BB in a canteen.
jokes.setup: Will describing Omar's stupidity
jokes.punchline: Visual metaphor with sound effect for empty-headedness
jokes.explanation: Vivid and creative metaphor for stupidity with visual comedy element. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Me and Carlton can take you. / You and who? / Carlton. Ain't that right...? Carlton?
jokes.setup: Will claims he and Carlton can fight Omar
jokes.punchline: Carlton has obviously fled, leaving Will alone
jokes.explanation: Classic abandonment comedy - Carlton's cowardly exit leaves Will in the lurch.

jokes.text: That's made from the part of the pig that's not good enough to go in sausage. / But it's good enough to go in Philip.
jokes.setup: Hilary's disgust about scrapple ingredients
jokes.punchline: Aunt Vy's dig at Uncle Phil's eating habits
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline structure with family roasting. Well-timed and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I don't know, I'm supposed to be in England. Get some English muffins.
jokes.setup: Being asked to shop for groceries
jokes.punchline: Hilary's only idea connects to her fake England trip
jokes.explanation: Good Hilary character moment - her self-centered thinking and tenuous logic.

jokes.text: There is room for one more.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey enjoying the luxury of the house to himself
jokes.punchline: Ominous invitation suggesting he's living it up
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's rare moment of rebellion, implied visual of him enjoying the house.

jokes.text: You're the chicken sandwich guy.
jokes.setup: Omar finally recognizing Will
jokes.punchline: Reducing Will to his embarrassing sandwich nickname
jokes.explanation: Effective callback to the sandwich naming, emphasizing Will's humiliation.

jokes.text: You so ugly, when you was born, the doctor just slapped both your parents.
jokes.setup: Will trying to provoke Omar into fighting
jokes.punchline: Creative twist on 'so ugly' jokes
jokes.explanation: Creative variation on classic insult format. Good structure and memorable punchline.

jokes.text: She is like a doorknob, everybody gets a turn!
jokes.setup: Will escalating his insult attempts
jokes.punchline: Crude but creative metaphor
jokes.explanation: Standard crude humor but with creative metaphor. Gets the job done in context.

jokes.text: I guess the mom's the red button.
jokes.setup: Omar not reacting to mother insults
jokes.punchline: Will's realization about what topic will provoke Omar
jokes.explanation: Good observational moment showing Will learning Omar's triggers.

jokes.text: That's my rep.
jokes.setup: Omar pointing to kids he helps in the community
jokes.punchline: Redefining reputation as positive community impact
jokes.explanation: Powerful moment redefining what reputation means. Economical and meaningful character development.

jokes.text: Maybe you can come back when they make it to the World Series. / I can't do it.
jokes.setup: Vy suggesting Will return for Phillies World Series
jokes.punchline: Will's immediate 'I can't do it' seemingly about the impossibility
jokes.explanation: Sports futility humor with misdirection - seems like Will can't wait that long

jokes.text: Maybe you can come back when they make it to the World Series. / I can't do it.
jokes.setup: Aunt Vy optimistically mentioning Phillies World Series
jokes.punchline: Will's immediate declaration of impossibility
jokes.explanation: Sports humor about the Phillies' prospects, though somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: Just watch it on TV with the rest of us.
jokes.setup: Will saying he can't make it to the World Series
jokes.punchline: Carlton's practical suggestion treating it as logistics issue
jokes.explanation: Carlton misunderstanding the emotional context, treating it purely practically.

jokes.text: You mean I was in a garbage can for nothing?
jokes.setup: Learning Will's reputation wasn't that bad
jokes.punchline: Carlton's realization his hiding was unnecessary
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy callback - Carlton's cowardly hiding was revealed to be excessive.

jokes.text: Look, when we get back, Dad will buy us a stoop.
jokes.setup: Will talking about missing sitting on the stoop
jokes.punchline: Carlton's solution is to purchase authentic experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - trying to buy authentic experiences. Brilliant character-specific humor.

jokes.text: I'm not.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asking when Will is coming back
jokes.punchline: Will's simple declaration that he's not returning
jokes.explanation: Dramatic cliffhanger ending with maximum economy - two words creating major impact
